skill.name: pdf-processing
skill.description: Extract text and tables from PDF files, work with forms, and plan common PDF document operations.
skill.summary: Guides PDF extraction, form handling, and document manipulation workflows.
skill.author: 0xkynz

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and appear to be false positives. The file contains PDF-processing guidance and a local pdfplumber example, with no confirmed shell execution, weak cryptography, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography in Metadata
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3 as weak cryptography, but the line is a natural-language description of PDF capabilities. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is frontmatter text describing PDF extraction, forms, and document extraction. I found no cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or related code at this location.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography in Body Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 8 as weak cryptography, but the line only states that the skill works with PDF documents. No weak cryptographic behavior is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is plain descriptive prose. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation in the file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Shell Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 14 as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the line starts a Markdown Python code block. The example imports pdfplumber and opens a local file path without invoking a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines show a fenced Python example, not Ruby backtick execution or command substitution. The snippet does not call subprocess, os.system, shell=True, or any external command runner.

content.value_statement: PDF work often requires careful extraction, form handling, and page operations. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical guidance for common PDF workflows.
actual_capabilities: Recommends pdfplumber for extracting text from PDF pages.
Describes extracting tables and converting them to CSV or JSON.
Covers PDF form filling and form field extraction workflows.
Lists document operations such as merge, split, rotate, and watermark.
Advises checking encryption and handling scanned documents with OCR.
limitations: Does not include implementation files beyond SKILL.md guidance.
Does not bundle PDF libraries or install dependencies automatically.
OCR support is mentioned but not implemented in the skill content.
Extraction quality depends on PDF structure and selected libraries.

use_cases.title: Extract Contract Text
use_cases.description: Pull readable text from contract PDFs before review, summarization, or comparison.

use_cases.title: Convert Report Tables
use_cases.description: Extract tables from reports and prepare them for spreadsheet or data analysis workflows.

use_cases.title: Prepare Form Documents
use_cases.description: Plan form field extraction, form filling, and routine page operations for document packets.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Text From One PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pdf-processing skill to extract text from this PDF. Identify any pages that appear scanned or unreadable.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Tables for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pdf-processing skill to extract tables from this PDF and describe the best export format for each table.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Form Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pdf-processing skill to inspect this fillable PDF and propose a workflow for extracting and filling form fields.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Batch PDF Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pdf-processing skill to design a batch workflow that splits, merges, rotates, and validates a folder of PDF documents.

output_examples.input: Extract the first page of a searchable invoice PDF.
output_examples.output: A text summary of the first page, with invoice number, vendor name, dates, totals, and any extraction warnings.

output_examples.input: Find tables in a quarterly financial report.
output_examples.output: A list of detected tables, suggested column names, page references, and recommended cleanup steps.

output_examples.input: Prepare three PDFs for a single client packet.
output_examples.output: A document operation plan covering merge order, page rotation checks, watermark placement, and validation steps.

best_practices: Check whether the PDF is encrypted before extraction or manipulation.
Use OCR only when pages are scanned images without selectable text.
Validate extracted text and tables against the source document.
anti_patterns: Do not assume table extraction is accurate without reviewing page layout.
Do not overwrite original PDFs before confirming the output file.
Do not use text extraction alone for scanned PDFs that require OCR.

faq.question: Does this skill process scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: It recommends handling OCR cases, but it does not provide a built-in OCR implementation.

faq.question: Can it extract tables from PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes table extraction and conversion to CSV or JSON.

faq.question: Can it fill PDF forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers form filling, form field extraction, and fillable form creation workflows.

faq.question: Does it merge and split PDF documents?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill lists merge, split, rotate, and watermark operations.

faq.question: Which libraries does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends pdfplumber for extraction and PyPDF2 for document manipulation.

faq.question: Does the skill send PDF data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. SKILL.md does not include network requests or external service integrations.
